operator_scope.semantic_signature: stop condition for legal hidden-correct pointwise kernels

valid_when: Repeated benchmark and profile summary consistently indicate memory_bound behavior.
Several low-risk schedule attempts have not produced a reproducible p50/p95 improvement beyond benchmark noise.
invalid_when: Profile indicates uncoalesced memory, register pressure, occupancy limit, atomics, or arithmetic bottleneck that has a targeted repair action.
preconditions: Do not use this stop capsule without benchmark_repeat and profile_attribution gates.

mechanism: For bandwidth-limited pointwise kernels, further arithmetic rewrites or large knob sweeps can waste budget once memory traffic is already close to the task lower bound; ECC-KB should preserve search budget for higher-value fixes.

instructions: Stop broad block-size or arithmetic-expression tuning when hidden correctness, repeated benchmark, and profile attribution all indicate a stable memory-bound plateau.
Before stopping, check only for obvious wasted bandwidth: unmasked over-read, non-coalesced contiguous operands, temporary tensor materialization, or accidental high-level fallback.
Do not convert this stop decision into a stable H20 claim unless benchmark distributions and profile refs are attached.
action.patch_hint: Record the stop reason in the run trace with candidate hash, p50/p95 distribution, profile summary, and attempted knob list.

expected_effect.performance: Avoids low-value tuning after a memory-bound plateau; does not assert the current kernel is optimal.
expected_effect.search_efficiency: Reduces token, time, and benchmark calls spent on unlikely improvements.
expected_effect.risk: Stopping too early can miss layout-specific coalescing fixes; profile attribution must be checked first.

performance_requirements: Require fixed warmup/repeat settings and p50/p95 report.
Require profile summary to distinguish memory_bound from uncoalesced memory or occupancy-limited.
validation.promotion_policy: Remain quarantine until multiple H20 traces show this stop rule reduces budget without losing best legal p50.

known_failures: Prematurely stopping before fixing non-coalesced accesses can lock in a poor layout kernel.
Using only a single benchmark sample can mistake noise for a plateau.
boundary_tests: profile summary changes after coalescing fix
benchmark variance high enough to mask small differences
do_not_apply: uncoalesced memory symptom with available coalescing action
